programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at stately home Anstey Hall in Cambridge, with father and son combo John and Johnny de Bruyne.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to clothes-free Clover Spa and Hotel in Birmingham where owner Tim Higgs hopes to convert his rivals to the joys of naturism.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to The Angel Hotel in Coleford, Gloucestershire, managed by Mark Davidson and Layla Caton.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to Higher Gitcombe, in Totnes, Devon, where owners Paul and Lynnie Jolly set out to spoil their guests rotten.

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's third visit is to Joe's grade II listed hotel in Lincolnshire, where a noisy night drives one guest to distraction.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Paul and Debbie's luxury BandB in Norfolk, pro chef Paul is determined to impress with his breakfast. But feedback from one guest sets him on the warpath.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's payment day, bringing with it backstabbing, blazing barneys, explosive confrontations and... little pink envelopes with cash in them. Who'll win the week?

programme.desc: Annette and Mark dream of finding the perfect holiday home with a pool on Spain's Orihuela Costa for £150k. Jasmine Harman's on hand to make sure the search goes swimmingly.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Lincolnshire, Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole meet Pete, whose former chicken sheds are stacked with unwanted stuff, including a toilet cistern and a dressing room mirror.

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.title: First Dates

programme.title: Belle Starr
programme.desc: Belle Starr is the daughter of a Southern aristocrat. When the invading Yankees take over her land, she vows revenge and heads to Missouri where she marries Confederate outlaw Sam Starr, and joins him and his gang in harrying the occupying troops. In fact, the real Belle Starr was a simple outlaw who, with her Cherokee husband, used the opportunity of the confusion of war to line their own pockets.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A dangerous drug dealer lets far more than the cat out of the bag, name swapping friends leave the interceptors baffled, and Phil has a shock for an illegal driver while Mac deals with a drunk drummer.

programme.desc: Concerned 999 callers report an HGV swerving all over the M6. PCs Jamie Barlow and Amy Hollis team up to search for the vehicle in question. When they finally catch up with the driver, they discover the awful truth behind his dangerous driving.

programme.title: Motorway: Hell on the Highway
programme.desc: How junctions can cause driving disasters, the dangers of joining a motorway, and a multi-car crash caused by a group of cars racing on a highway in New York at night time.

programme.title: The X-Files

programme.sub-title: The One With the Free Porn

programme.desc: Ross tries to keep Emily from returning to London, and Monica and Rachel attempt to win back their apartment.

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Chris takes on some elusive mice causing havoc in an unexpected location. In Buckinghamshire, a lorry load of potentially hazardous waste has been dumped by the side of a main road.

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Chloe is taken under the wing of enforcers Ian and Anneil as they conduct a street sweep in the London Borough of Hounslow. In Haringey, grime fighters in the borough use more than a hundred cameras to monitor their eleven square mile patch.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The cops have their hands full with night time drink driving incidents. Four police cars are needed to stop a car with a motorbike hanging out of the boot.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Illegal drivers are a menace on UK roads. The traffic cops hunt down disqualified, unlicensed and uninsured drivers.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The cops are called to a fatal accident near a theme park, where they suspect the driver responsible for the crash has been using drugs.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: After a pensioner gets knocked down, the traffic cops lead an urgent city wide search for a suspected hit and run driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Road Crimes Team pursue a 4x4 connected to fuel theft, interceptors battle to stop a dangerous driver a stolen lorry, and firearms cops are forced to use their big red key.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A van driver blocks himself into a dead end and repeatedly rams the interceptors' car, a self-confident drink driver gets argumentative, and reports arrive of a wanted vehicle travelling towards the Nottinghamshire border.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim and Lisa direct an intelligence-led strike. Suspects are on the move; it's believed they're in possession of a handgun and ammunition. The Knife Crime Team target drug hotspots.

programme.desc: Leo faces a life-threatening crisis: his liver is failing and he needs an urgent transplant. Daniel needs surgery for a tumour that could make its way to his lungs.

programme.title: A&E After Dark

programme.title: Accused: Guilty or Innocent?
programme.desc: In 2021, Taio Cruz was accused of breaking into a house and assaulting the occupants. Having taken a plea deal, the 24-year-old could be sent to jail. Will she show remorse and take responsibility for her actions or is she playing the system?

programme.desc: The loveable animal friends as they visit Lebanon. Bashir loves his cherry tree. Following the pit trail to find out who ate Bashir's cherries and how the magical pits turn into many cherry trees.

programme.desc: Two cars and a road interact and find their friendship tested in each episode. Trucky is responsible, Voom Vam is adventurous and Roady is always worried: together they experience adventures, ordeals and sometimes funny misunderstandings.

programme.title: Walter & Dude
programme.desc: Walter and Dude are looking for an item that you wear when you are ready to go to bed. Is it a teddy bear, a coat or a night lamp? Join them as they discover pyjamas!

programme.desc: Make your child a star on BabyTV by creating their very own personalized birthday greeting. Using a digital photo, parents can create a clip for their child which can be viewed online or submitted to BabyTV for the chance of being featured on-air.

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes
programme.desc: Sing along with classics `I'm a little teapot', `Baa Baa Black sheep' and `Itsy Bitsy Spider'.

programme.title: Bath Tubbies
programme.desc: Froggy is blowing soap bubbles in the bathtub! Who's spraying water on him? Ellie is playing ball and Ducky is sleeping on a mattress! Who could it be? Come along and find out!

programme.title: Draco
programme.desc: Join Draco as he learns the difference between opposites - dirty and clean. After getting dirty from playing in the mud, Draco takes a good long bath and comes out clean. He even gives his cute teddy bear a bath and they both are clean as can be!

programme.title: The World Is A Wonder
programme.desc: Come and explore the life cycle of a chicken! Join the hen with the red comb as she meets a fine rooster! After he's gone, the hen lays three eggs! She sits on them to guard them and keep them warm!

programme.title: Zeina's Detective Agency
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Brave Basket
programme.desc: A highly familiar day-to-day object arrives at Zeina's doorstep forgetting what it is used for. Searching for the answer, Zeina and her best friend Roy come up with a new and hilarious use for the surprised object.

programme.title: Welcome to the Island
programme.desc: Coco, the wise coconut tree, Bucky, the curious rock, Lily, the little flower, and Blossom, the happy papaya tree, explore themes of fostering and building friendships while having fun on their small island.

programme.title: Teeny and Tiny's Classroom
programme.desc: Teeny and Tiny are two happy and mischievous friends that share with us their riddles and games. As we follow the antics of the tiny friends, we discover shapes, colours and quantities.

programme.title: Mike's Bike
programme.desc: Mikey is the owner of a local bike store where he repairs his friends' bikes. Mikey always knows what to do, even if it takes him a few tries to get it right. In the process, he comes up with unique and funny ideas.

programme.title: Stick With Mick
programme.desc: Mick gets ready to go hiking. He grabs a pair of hiking boots and a water bottle.

programme.desc: Crafty Rafty creates a crab from a sock with button eyes and pin claws.

programme.desc: Animated series based on Rudyard Kipling's classic about the adventures of Mowgli.

programme.title: The Lollipops Song Series
programme.desc: Three toddler friends who form a girl band and turn everyday ups and downs into fun.

programme.desc: Henry wants to drink carrot juice but Chef George is out of carrots. Join Hungry Henry on his journey to find carrots. There aren't any at the grocery store or in the market, but there are carrots in the field! Come along, pick some carrots and enjoy tasty carrot juice with Henry!

programme.title: Mice Builders
programme.desc: Mice are devoted to building their new house.

programme.desc: Pix and Leo enjoy fun in the snow! From building a snowman to having a snowball fight, snowboarding and even ice skating, Pix and Leo have a ball in the snow!

programme.desc: Kim is a girl, Kit is her robot and together they have fun finding out about the world; Kit learns to play in the sand, paint pictures and grow plants; Kim learns new things about her robot.

programme.desc: Walter the Mouse and Dude the Dog need help to find an item they have misplaced.

programme.title: Mona & Sketch

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the 2025/26 FA Cup final between Chelsea and Manchester City from Wembley Stadium. Manchester City are looking to win the FA Cup for the third time under Pep Guardiola after lifting the famous old trophy in 2019 and 2023. Chelsea's last FA Cup success was in 2018, and they've lost all three finals they've played in since then. This is the first time these two famous clubs have faced each other in an FA Cup final.

programme.title: Weatherman Walking

On his journey he rings an ancient church bell, finds a water scorpion which breathes through its bottom, and learns about the `Rogiet Hoard' and discovers how the village of Sudbrook evolved. He also wades into the River Severn in the footsteps of ancient fisherman and finishes in Chepstow.

programme.desc: On 6 July 1988, a series of explosions destroyed the Piper Alpha oil platform, with 167 men killed and only 61 survivors. It remains the world's worst offshore disaster. Follow the story of how the disaster unfolded.

programme.title: The Harbour
programme.desc: Today, trainee harbour pilot Ralph Greig faces a tough test on the simulator on his first day of training. Meanwhile, ex-sailor Ellen Haughland has her heart set on stepping into Ralph's shoes as a VTS controller - can she become Aberdeen's first ever woman in that role? Onboard the Bibby Sapphire, the crew brace themselves for a force ten storm heading directly towards them.

programme.title: The Harbour
programme.desc: In this edition, fish merchant Doug Rennie contends with a move to a new state-of-the-art fish processing factory. Plus, the Highland Prince loads up with vital supplies for a North Sea oil rig, new pilot Ralph Greig's training illustrates why he has one of the most dangerous jobs in the harbour, and the harbour chaplain's tireless work to open a sea farer's centre in Aberdeen finally pays off.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons face a difficult dilemma in the middle of a life-saving bile duct cancer operation. A chest sarcoma proves more challenging than expected, with no room for error.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: With Ken needing to re-sit his driving exam in order to win back his licence, Richard offers to take him out on a mock test, not realising just how rusty his dad has become behind the wheel.

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: After an emotional funeral for his dad, Colin and Cathy have the neighbours round but understandably Colin is not his usual upbeat self. Christine was not impressed with the service or the priest and, as usual, is not backward about coming forward with her opinions on the whole affair. And as Beth, Eric and Ian try to make their excuses to leave early, Cathy manages to foil their escape by inviting more of the neighbours in.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex Batty vanished on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Then, six years later, a teenager appeared on the side of a road in the French Pyrenees and told a passing driver that he'd been kidnapped. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.desc: A mother tells a lie for her beloved son that rips apart their family and close-knit Irish town -- ultimately testing her own sense of right and wrong.

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: Kidnapped and held in a remote farmhouse, and with Black Death's auction looming, Chloe realises she must find a way to gain control over her captor.

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: After escaping captivity, Chloe Ayling faces a new ordeal - media scrutiny, public doubt and a relentless fight to be believed as the truth behind her kidnap begins to unfold.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Birmingham's Hockley Social Club has been transformed into our very own comedy club. Taking to the stage are Eli Hart, Farhan Solo, Chloe Reynolds, Will BF and Monica Hsueh in a bid to impress our judges and book their place in the final. Award-winning comedian and writer Sarah Keyworth is here to keep the house in order. The judges are comedian, writer and podcaster Aurie Styla and local Brum stand-up Lindsey Santoro. Head judge Fatiha El-Ghorri completes the trio.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Adam Henson visit Oswestry Livestock Market, one of the region's largest weekly cattle and sheep markets. As spring trading peaks, they step into a fast-moving world where fortunes can change in minutes. With livestock numbers falling and markets closing nationwide, Oswestry remains a crucial lifeline, not just for trade, but for farming communities to come together.

En route, the girls are bedevilled by private detective Malone, hired by Esmond's father to make certain that Lorelei isn't just another gold-digger. When Dorothy falls in love with the poverty-stricken Malone, Lorelei decides to find her pal a wealthier potential husband.

programme.title: Bing

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee thought his tie was a little bit plain, so he added some accessories and now it looks amazing. The Squirrels admire Duggee's new look and want to learn how to accessorise themselves. The first step for the Squirrels is to find something they'd like to change. The Squirrels each bring a different item: wellies, a T-shirt, headband, helmet and a potato. They grab sequins, patches, feathers and lights, and then showcase what they have all done.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee masters his ambition of building a model of the Clubhouse out of sticks, so the Squirrels start to think about what their ambitions might be. Norrie wants to learn how to break dance. Happy has always wanted to jump the famous triple puddle splash. Betty has always wanted to teach Mr Cuddles how to speak Portuguese. Roly has loads, from growing a potato to becoming a pirate. Tag spends a long time thinking what his could be, and then it clicks - he needs to climb over the log!

programme.title: Zog and the Flying Doctors

It shows her on holiday as a child in a Spanish town with her long-lost friend Maria. Foz suggests they visit Maria, but there's a problem: Xuli was so young when she used to holiday there that she can't remember where it was! Luckily, Foz's keen eyes quickly spot an important clue, and soon they're searching across Spain in hope of a reunion.

Perhaps the final mega monster make will help - a bottle street.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Late Thanksgiving

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Birth Mother
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler are forced to pretend to be a reverend and a doctor when their prospective child's birth mother gets the wrong end of the stick. Meanwhile, Joey's date with Phoebe's friend doesn't go well, and Ross is left with an inappropriate outfit for a date with a sexy salesgirl.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Gets Caught
programme.desc: Phoebe and Rachel see Chandler getting into a car with a mystery woman and follow them to a large house in the suburbs. They tell Monica of their fears and are shocked by the truth.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where the Stripper Cries
programme.desc: Joey appears as a celebrity contestant on `The $100,000 Pyramid' with its actual host Donny Osmond, but Joey's dubious IQ costs his partner the prize money. Meanwhile, Monica and Rachel hire an ungainly stripper, Roy - without seeing him first - for Phoebe's bachelorette party. Also, Chandler and Ross attend a college reunion and meet an attractive woman they both promised not to date during school.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Speaks French
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler discover that their prospective adoptive child's biological father may be a `shovel killer', Ross and Rachel get dangerously close when they go to visit her sick father, and Phoebe struggles to teach Joey French for an audition.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel makes the mistake of going for a job interview at a restaurant where her current boss is also dining. Phoebe discovers she can change her name to whatever she likes - Princess Consuela Bananahammock seems the logical choice, until Mike proves that two can play her game - and Joey, upset that Chandler and Monica are moving, is comforted by an eight-year-old.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Rachel makes a startling announcement, Ross secretly schemes to get her former job back for her. Meanwhile, Joey is so flustered by all of the upheaval in his friends' lives that Phoebe prevents him from learning about the sudden death of his agent. Monica and Chandler make a stunning discovery after they check out the house for sale next door to their new home.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's Going Away Party
programme.desc: As Rachel prepares to leave for Paris, the gang throw a farewell party for her. Instead of facing them all together, she takes each of them aside for a private goodbye. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The six friends prepare to say goodbye to each other.

programme.title: Dr. Dolittle 2
programme.desc: A group of beavers, headed by a Godfather-style leader, ask Dolittle to save their habitat from loggers. The only hope is to get the forest preserved because it's the home of a protected bear, but there's a problem: the bear's the only bear in the forest, so she can't reproduce. Undaunted, Dolittle persuades a circus bear to help out, but he has to teach him not just the ways of the wild, but the wiles of lady bears too.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre hosts as Katherine Ryan, Asim Chaudhry, Vanessa Feltz, Steve Pemberton, Kate Garraway, Ainsley Harriott and Phil Tufnell try to help the contestants answer their way to a fortune.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The guys conduct interviews for DNA testing and cause mischief at the grocery store while wearing bath robes. Also, the big loser cracks under pressure during a presentation at a children's museum.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where It All Began
programme.desc: An introduction to the gang. After Rachel leaves her Mr Potato Head look-alike fiancé Barry at the altar, she moves in with Monica and discovers that independence sucks when you don't have Daddy's credit cards to rely on. Meanwhile, Chandler and Joey encourage Ross to experience the advantages of bachelorhood, and Phoebe supports herself by playing guitar in the subway.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Sonogram at the End
programme.desc: Ross's lesbian ex-wife is pregnant with his child and he doesn't like her choice of last name for the baby. Elsewhere, Rachel finds out that her ex-fiancé went on what would have been their honeymoon with her maid of honour.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica finds it difficult to break up with her latest boyfriend whom everyone likes, Chandler is reunited with his beloved cigarettes, and Phoebe receives a large sum of money.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: While the men attend a hockey game and end up at a hospital, the women spy on President Clinton's top aide, George Stephanopoulos, who lives across the street. Meanwhile, Rachel receives her first pay packet.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the East German Laundry Detergent
programme.desc: While Chandler and Phoebe decide to break up with their significant others on the same night, Ross and Rachel have their first date doing laundry together.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: An agent offers Joey a movie role as Al Pacino's naked body double, Ross tries to be less conservative, and Chandler thinks his fantasies have come true when he has an affair with a married woman.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A power cut leaves Chandler stranded in a bank with a gorgeous woman, Ross tries to ask Rachel out on a date, and Joey learns that Monica used to be infatuated with him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Nana Dies Twice
programme.desc: Monica and Ross mourn the death of their grandmother with a little help from the gang, and Monica and her mum come to an unspoken understanding about the relationship between mothers and daughters. Meanwhile, Chandler is irritated by office speculation that he is gay.

programme.title: See No Evil, Hear No Evil
programme.desc: Lighthearted adventures of a pair of news vendors in New York - one deaf, one blind - who go on the run when they are blamed for the murder of a customer. Each has seen - or heard - just a whiff of a clue, and a frantic chase ensues as the duo struggle to clear their names.

programme.title: Police Squad!
programme.desc: Two men are found murdered in the offices of Acme Finance Credit Union.

programme.title: Police Squad!
programme.desc: Bumbling detectives solve puzzling cases filled with sight gags.

After Rachel leaves her Mr Potato Head look-alike fiancé Barry at the altar, she moves in with Monica and discovers that independence sucks when you don't have Daddy's credit cards to rely on. Meanwhile, Chandler and Joey encourage Ross to experience the advantages of bachelorhood, and Phoebe supports herself by playing guitar in the subway.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Sonogram at the End
programme.desc: Ross's lesbian ex-wife is pregnant with his child and he doesn't like her choice of last name for the baby. Elsewhere, Rachel finds out that her ex-fiancé went on what would have been their honeymoon with her maid of honour.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica finds it difficult to break up with her latest boyfriend whom everyone likes, Chandler is reunited with his beloved cigarettes, and Phoebe receives a large sum of money.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: While the men attend a hockey game and end up at a hospital, the women spy on President Clinton's top aide, George Stephanopoulos, who lives across the street. Meanwhile, Rachel receives her first pay packet.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the East German Laundry Detergent
programme.desc: While Chandler and Phoebe decide to break up with their significant others on the same night, Ross and Rachel have their first date doing laundry together.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: An agent offers Joey a movie role as Al Pacino's naked body double, Ross tries to be less conservative, and Chandler thinks his fantasies have come true when he has an affair with a married woman.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A power cut leaves Chandler stranded in a bank with a gorgeous woman, Ross tries to ask Rachel out on a date, and Joey learns that Monica used to be infatuated with him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Nana Dies Twice
programme.desc: Monica and Ross mourn the death of their grandmother with a little help from the gang, and Monica and her mum come to an unspoken understanding about the relationship between mothers and daughters. Meanwhile, Chandler is irritated by office speculation that he is gay.

programme.title: See No Evil, Hear No Evil
programme.desc: Lighthearted adventures of a pair of news vendors in New York - one deaf, one blind - who go on the run when they are blamed for the murder of a customer. Each has seen - or heard - just a whiff of a clue, and a frantic chase ensues as the duo struggle to clear their names.

programme.title: Police Squad!
programme.desc: Two men are found murdered in the offices of Acme Finance Credit Union.

programme.title: Police Squad!
programme.desc: Bumbling detectives solve puzzling cases filled with sight gags.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson return for a new series of `8 Out of 10 Cats'. Jimmy once again oversees the disputes and deliberations on the topical events that set the nation's chins wagging as Jon and Sean are joined by Joe Wilkinson, Ellie Taylor, Natalie Cassidy and Gabby Logan.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr once again oversees the disputes and deliberations on the topical events that set the nation's chins wagging as Jon and Sean are joined by guests Hannibal Buress, Sam Buttery, Stephen Mangan and Katherine Ryan.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: We're scouting for the next child star while trying not to laugh as Dan Jost (a family friend) reads his honest movie reviews in a focus group. Sadly, one of them will be crowned this week's loser, and will be forced to bite the hand that feeds him!

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Actress Emily Bett Rickards and comic Gary Anthony Williams join the regulars for skits, including Scenes from a Hat.

programme.title: Russell Howard: Right Here Right Now
programme.desc: Russell Howard, star of `Russell Howard's Good News' sees his nationwide sell-out, three times-extended live stand up show `Right Here, Right Now' broadcast on television for the first time on BBC3.

programme.title: Dragony Aunts
programme.desc: The Dragony Aunts answer some self-image questions. Jordan Gray wants to become 10% more fabulous onstage, while Kaily has a question about getting plastic surgery.

programme.title: Teachers
programme.desc: A neighbour moves in next door to Fillmore, but his proud ownership of a dangerous item is concerning to the Teachers. Ms Watson, fresh off her breakup with Toby, finally has to face him.

programme.title: Teachers
programme.desc: A neighbour moves in next door to Fillmore, but his proud ownership of a dangerous item is concerning to the Teachers. Ms Watson, fresh off her breakup with Toby, finally has to face him.

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn
programme.desc: When a 15-year-old girl vanishes from her front porch, police must uncover when she was last seen, where she might have been going, and who would have wanted her dead.

programme.desc: Two masked gunmen commit a violent robbery and shoot Michael Temple Jr., rendering him a quadriplegic for five years before his death; in this unusual cold case, DNA from a knife is used to create a sketch of the killer.

programme.desc: David Dickinson is in Loughborough for today's edition of the antiques show with Simon Schneider, Mark Stevens, Michael Hogben and Jan Keyne. programme.desc: The Clermont Diggers face pressure to repay $15,000 worth of gold or risk losing their mine. Gold Snipers Rob and Levi battle the Tasmanian wilderness to access a secret river system. And the Mineshaft Hunters enter an abandoned gold mine.

programme.desc: Businessman and rookie miner Dennis gambles $500,000 on a 6-acre gold mine, while Leigh asks two British backpackers for a lesson in metal detecting.

programme.title: Adventure Gold Diggers

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The four-wheel-drive Freelander was once Europe's most popular car. It looks good and runs well, but it failed the inspection on so many points that its owner gave up.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The team acquire a Triumph Herald that has only had two owners. If the engine, brakes, and gear can be repaired, it will be a gem.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will plans a mature dinner party to celebrate his birthday. Unfortunately, his party coincides with a far more popular one and the boys decide to crash it.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: In a desperate attempt at reinvention, Will decides that the lads should be cool, and become `the boys who go to clubs'. Simon is not so sure, but when Carli thinks it's cool he immediately changes his mind.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Pash covers William's cheeky self-portrait and Uzzi gives Josh a helping hand. Meanwhile, Sketch loses Leanne's soap opera slip-up. Plus, Alice inks world champion toe wrestler Alan.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Nick Mohammed sheathes a water bottle with a sock, Sophie Willan attempts to take flight, and John Robins puts his faith in honey, as Taskmaster Greg Davies sits in judgement.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, as team captain Sean Lock and Kevin Bridges are up against Jon Richardson and Kathy Burke, with Joe Lycett in Dictionary Corner, while Nick Helm `helps' Rachel Riley with the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits the kitchens of six restaurants that he altered when they were about to close their doors.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to America in a bid to rescue failing restaurant owners from imminent disaster and provide them with a recipe for success. In this first episode, Gordon visits the Hot Potato Café in Fishtown, Philadelphia. With no profit made in its two years of trading and an influential restaurant reviewer dubbing them `Spuddy Hell', Gordon soon realises Hot Potato may have had its chips.

programme.title: Hollyoaks: Omnibus
programme.desc: Frankie wants to come clean to Caleb, while Charlie's crumbling under the weight of his lies. The village gathers for Rex and Ste's wedding. And Diane reflects on her life in Hollyoaks.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: It's Valentine's Day and, while Phil and Claire plan a special trip, Jay woos Gloria by taking her to a stand-up show. Expect varying romantic results.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Celebrating the freaky, frenetic and funny side of family life.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Celebrating the freaky, frenetic and funny side of family life.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell and Manny find out they've got more in common than they thought, while Cameron and Gloria spend some quality time together.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits the kitchens of six restaurants that he altered when they were about to close their doors.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon helps Peter's Italian Restaurant in Babylon, New York where the peace of Long Island shore is regularly shattered by the antics of the volatile Pellegrino family, who run the restaurant.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The dust is settling on three life-changing months. But not before the experts seek clarity on the burning issues one more time.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The dust has settled on three life-changing months. But not before the experts seek clarity on the burning issues one final time, with some heartbreaking truths laid bare.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Revenge Is a Dish Best Served Three Times
programme.desc: Homer is accused of treason in post-revolutionary France, Milhouse leads a nerd rebellion, and mysterious crimefighter `Red Revenge' takes to the streets.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart finds himself tied down with a baby on the way when he falls for Natalie Portman.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer decides he doesn't need church and forms his own religion.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa wins second prize in a beauty contest. When the winner is miraculously hit by lightning Lisa takes over, only to find that one of her duties is being a spokesperson for a cigarette company.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons throw a Halloween party where Lisa, Grampa and Bart each tell a scary story. A Krusty the Klown doll comes alive and terrorises Homer, Montgomery Burns travels to Skull Island to catch giant ape `King Homer', and Bart accidentally wakes all the human dead in Springfield.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer sells a Joe DiMaggio card for a pile of cash, then fritters it all away before being roused by Charles Dickens' `A Christmas Carol'.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Marge vs. Singles, Seniors, Childless Couples and Teens and Gays
programme.desc: A baby riot breaks out at a children's concert in Springfield, so the town's childless community form an anti-youth lobby. The initiative threatens to eliminate all child-friendly aspects of the city, until Marge decides to fight back with a cunning plan of her own.

programme.title: The Amazing Race Australia: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: On beautiful Borneo, the celebs get hands-on gutting fish and dive headfirst into a conservation mission. But saving the planet takes a toll on our eco warriors.

programme.title: I, Robot
programme.desc: Set in 2035, Chicago cop Del Spooner doesn't trust the robots that are part of human society. They're supposed to be harmless but Spooner's latest murder hunt unearths a frightening android conspiracy. Only the robotics expert Susan Calvin can help him fight the growing danger, but first he has to convince her that her precious robots can do harm.

programme.title: White House Down
programme.desc: While on a tour of the White House with his young daughter, a police officer springs into action to save his child and the president from a heavily armed group of paramilitary invaders.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: What do Britain's favourite TV viewers think of 2021's biggest shows, from Squid Game to Strictly and Line of Duty? Including the Siddiqui family, Giles and Mary, and Jenny and Lee.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Luke, who's lost his dating mojo, is raring to reinvigorate his love life and find a lady to relight his fire. And can pole dancer Abi find someone to send her heart into a spin?

programme.title: Robin and Marian
programme.desc: The delightful tale of Robin Hood and Maid Marian in their twilight years. Robin, back from the Crusades, finds his love has become the Abbess of a priory and that his arch enemy, the Sheriff of Nottingham still wants him dead.

programme.title: The Story of Robin Hood and His Merrie Men
programme.desc: Robin Hood, the dashing rogue, embarks on a legendary campaign against the evil Prince John and the Sheriff of Nottingham.

programme.title: Romancing the Stone
programme.desc: A dowdy romantic-adventure writer is hurled into a real-life adventure in the Colombian jungle in order to save her sister, who will be killed if a treasure map is not delivered to her captors. She is helped out by a brash mercenary, and together they search for the priceless gem located in the map.

programme.title: A Room With a View
programme.desc: On holiday in Florence, Charlotte Bartlett and her cousin encounter Mr Emerson, an English tourist accompanied by his son. After an unpleasant incident, the two women return to England but when Mr Emerson turns up as a tenant of a nearby house, all sorts of romantic complications ensue.

programme.title: Horizon Line
programme.desc: A former couple board a single-engine plane for a routine and casual flight to their friend's tropical island wedding. But within minutes after takeoff, their pilot suffers a fatal heart attack, leaving them with no clue how to land the plane.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: Journalist Eddie Brock is trying to take down Carlton Drake, the notorious and brilliant founder of the Life Foundation. While investigating one of Drake's experiments, Eddie's body merges with the alien Venom -- leaving him with superhuman strength and power. Twisted, dark and fueled by rage, Venom tries to control the new and dangerous abilities that Eddie finds so intoxicating.

programme.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage
programme.desc: Eddie Brock is still struggling to coexist with the shape-shifting extraterrestrial Venom. When deranged serial killer Cletus Kasady also becomes host to an alien symbiote, Brock and Venom must put aside their differences to stop his reign of terror.

programme.title: Millie Lies Low
programme.desc: Anxiety-ridden Millie starts to travel from Wellington to New York, but she panics, causing her to miss her flight.

programme.desc: In California, Joe Mantegna joins the party for a righteous ricotta pie. A Cincinnati joint is plating a prime-time pork burger with kimchi, and a spot in Austin, TX, is serving killer coconut escolar ceviche.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri road trips across America to check out classic greasy spoons. With a new generation of owners and delicious homemade food, these are the kind of places that make America special.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Local country favorites.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Viewer suggestions for the best diners and drive-ins.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam devours a maple doughnut smothered with bacon and competes in the kitchen sink challenge, which means finishing two gallons of ice cream with eight toppings and whipped cream.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam tries a monster Burritozilla and then competes in a super-spicy challenge, the Special Number Two, where he must finish a fiery ramen soup that even the chef won't try.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam's culinary quest brings him to Albuquerque, New Mexico, where roasted green chillis are the condiment of choice. Adam's first stop is the Frontier Restaurant.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's quest for good eats lands him in Tampa, Florida - land of sunny skies and authentic flavours. Adam stops by Skipper's Smokehouse to try a Floridian speciality - alligator ribs.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam visits his alma mater in New Haven, Conn., to find the origin of hamburgers; the Cheese Truck Challenge.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: A showcase of some of Adam's greatest pizza moments. Adam bites into a Reuben pizza covered in Swiss cheese and competes in the stuffed pizza challenge, in which he must finish 10lbs of pizza or join the wall of shame.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam bites into a chicken and cheddar biscuit sandwich, and then takes on the Ludicrous Wings Challenge, where he has 15 minutes to finish a dozen jumbo wings soaked in super spicy sauce.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's countrywide culinary quest has brought him to Harlem, New York. Harlem has been a major centre for New York's Latino and African-American communities for over a century.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam cheers on the Lo Cash Cowboys in the Rooster Challenge at Roosters Texas Style BBQ.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's edible escapade has brought him back to the Sooner State for a taste of Tulsa, Oklahoma. First, Adam heads to the Spudder, a local steakhouse.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: Chef James Martin prepares cakes for special occasions - black forest gateau and a white chocolate celebration cake.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: James Martin presents some fantastic recipes to delight the sweetest of teeth with fantastic tart ideas: custard tart, treacle tart, a prune and almond tart.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: One of Britain's premier pastry and pudding makers - James Martin prepares vanilla and meringue ice cream, instant banana ice cream and mango sorbet.

programme.title: James Martin: Sweet
programme.desc: Here, festive desserts come under the spotlight, with parkin, vanilla fudge, an Easter egg and cranachan.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Spice Week and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith set the bakers a classic teatime Signature. There's also an Arabian Technical and a fiddly Showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It is Pastry Week, and Paul and Prue are looking for perfection with savoury and sweet samosas, a classic French Technical, and for the final challenge, a daunting majestic pie fit for a banquet.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It is a Bake Off first with Vegan week. Prue and Paul have set three very different challenges to test the remaining bakers including a savoury pastry Signature without butter, a cracking Technical, and a risky but spectacular Showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Velkommen to Danish Week with a bread Signature requiring style and substance, a spherical Technical, and an elaborate pastry Showstopper fit for a Danish birthday party and everyone is out to impress.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It is the semi-final and the remaining bakers are just three patisserie challenges away from the biggest day in their baking lives. The heat is really on now with the complex Signature, Technical and Showstopper challenges.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve bakers started. Now just three remain but only one can win. Paul and Prue set three challenges that will test the bakers' skills, including an intricate, multi-layered, edible landscape Showstopper that could win them the title.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Football
programme.desc: Adam is on the south coast to watch Brighton and Hove Albion. First up are incredible Mexican tacos, then a gourmet monkfish and triple cooked chips.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Football
programme.desc: Adam Richman combines his two great passions - food and football. He visits Britain's most famous teams and seeks out footie's finest foods!

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam takes on the hottest curry in the country at New York's Brick Lane Curry House.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman heads to New Orleans, where he takes on a plate of 15 dozen oysters at Acme's Oyster House. Did he remember to bring a knife?

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James' adventure brings him over the border into North Wales, where he's joined by chef Stephen Terry for this leg of his journey. Their first stop is the grand Pale Hall, where head chef Gareth Stevenson is cooking up a real treat - Moroccan-inspired Welsh lamb. James offers his own variation of Welsh lamb in the grounds of the hall, serving with gnocchi and vegetables. Next, one of James' boyhood dreams comes true as he has the ultimate falconry experience.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Stephen head south through Wales en route to Pembrokeshire. But before they get there, they stop to visit Gareth Ward and his stunning restaurant Ynyshir. After filling their bellies, they make it to the coast where James cooks up his Marloes Sands seafood parcel. Stephen tracks down Wales' answer to Willy Wonka, young chocolatier Liam Burgess, who helps him make a very special one-off-chocolate bar for James.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman heads to New Orleans, where he takes on a plate of 15 dozen oysters at Acme's Oyster House. Did he remember to bring a knife?

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: Nigel Slater looks at how spicy flavours work so well with something cooling. From a classic chicken curry to a spicy tomato stew with yoghurt, Nigel shares some of his favourite dishes, and to prove just how versatile this food pairing is, he cooks up a new version of a traditional pudding. Nigel also travels to Bristol to meet a man who is passionate about pepper.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: Nigel Slater continues his exploration of brilliant culinary combinations, this time turning his attention to texture. Success in the kitchen is not just about tastes and flavours; contrasting textures can really lift a dish. He makes a classic savoury pie with a puff pastry crust, and a reworking of his favourite childhood pudding: poached pears.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: The Drummond drive-through is open for business, and Ree Drummond is serving up the most sensational packed lunch known to cowboys. She starts with ranch-hand sandwiches filled with pulled pork and a side of sweet potato crisps.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is hosting brunch, and she's making things easy with a make-ahead party spread that's ready and in the fridge. First, she pulls together all the ingredients for an interactive and customised Bloody Mary bar.

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a simple but showstopping sticky onion tart; a creamy coconut pumpkin rice, with a spicy butter bean stew; and turbo-charged aubergines with epic Indian spices.

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals
programme.desc: Jamie makes a beautiful oozy risotto. In Jerusalem, Jamie is inspired by gorgeous breakfasts, and rustles up pancakes with spinach and chilli.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Turn Up the Volume

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina Garten shares a collection of restaurant-inspired dishes to cook at home. Ina cooks with Grand Pellegrino for the famous Rao's restaurant and finds out the best kitchens in America.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Biscuit Week and the bakers make a sophisticated fruit, nut and chocolate signature, produce two tropical technicals, and create an elaborate table setting showstopper.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: For Mary's outdoor party the dishes are best served cold; salmon fillets with herb sauce, ham with homemade chutney and fillet of beef with garlic and mustard cream. To accompany the cold meats and fish there are two salads.

programme.title: James Martin's Mediterranean
programme.desc: Join James Martin as he sails around the Med in search of stunning views and spectacular food. In Chania in Crete, he serves up his take on chicken souvlaki.

programme.title: James Martin's Mediterranean
programme.desc: More great Mediterranean food as James visits the beautiful town of Minori on Italy's Amalfi coast, where he gets a crash course in pasta making.

programme.title: James Martin's Favourite Feasts
programme.desc: Chef James Martin creates a special Christmas menu. He serves up figs and apple chutney, Yorkshire pot with creamed leeks, stollen cake and biscotti.

programme.title: James Martin's Favourite Feasts
programme.desc: Chef James Martin creates a menu using traditional game animals. He serves up a leaping salmon with cucumber pickle and then saddle of venison with spicy redcurrant jelly.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Football
programme.desc: Adam Richman combines his two great passions - food and football. He visits Britain's most famous teams and seeks out footie's finest foods!

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Football
programme.desc: Adam Richman combines his two great passions, food and football. He visits Britain's most famous teams and seeks out footie's finest foods.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: Michel arrives in London, where he goes mud larking and visits a 500-year-old pub. He cooks pan roast chicken, mussel stew and pear and plum crumble.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Classic burger and dog joints including stops in Baltimore, Minneapolis, and Austin, Texas.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy discovers local favorites in Tennessee, Virginia, and Nebraska.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Great food in unexpected places.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's eating adventure has brought him to beautiful Mobile, Alabama. First, Adam heads over to the Hungry Owl to try a burger that Mobile natives say is the 'best in 'Bama'.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam's culinary crusade takes him on a road trip along the beautiful Gulf Coast in search of delicious eats. Adam begins his journey at The Shed in Ocean Springs, Mississippi.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy takes a trip to the Seaport Museum in New York to get inspiration for a nautical-themed cake. Meanwhile, a celebrity stops by Carlo's to order a cake for her mum.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Sweet Sixteen, Stars & a Saber Sword
programme.desc: Buddy makes a cake for the 100th anniversary of the Cadet Chapel at West Point. He also makes a Hollywood-themed cake for Mauro's daughter's red carpet Sweet 16 party.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Central Region chefs - newcomer Pip Lacey, 4th time competitor Richard Bainbridge and returning chef Jason Hodnett - fight it out for the fish course. Pip is hoping to outclass her rivals with a smoked haddock dish inspired by her Grandma.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's a battle for the main course with three Central Region chefs - Pip Lacey, Richard Bainbridge and Jason Hodnett. Once again Jason is taking risks with his unusual collection of ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the Central Region chefs to impress, with the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges so with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central Region chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. They'll be joined by former chair of the Women's Institute Helen Carey, who will help decide who will have the glory of representing the region in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the starter course, and 8 of the finest chefs in the country are battling it out to plate up impeccable dishes worthy of the Women's Institute. The judges are joined by WI Calendar Girl Angela Baker, but after Oliver reveals that this year it will be much tougher to make it onto the judge's shortlist, who will keep their cool and secure a place?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the battle for the fish course, and those chefs who didn't make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by a WI president, Kristy Bowen, who knows the impeccable standards the Institute will expect.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's main course day, the half-way point in Finals Week, and those chefs who have yet to get a dish on the shortlist are feeling the heat. With only dishes judged to be perfect in with a chance at reaching the WI banquet, the standards are higher than ever before.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's a battle for dessert, and the last chance for the 8 regional champions to get a dish in contention for the final banquet. Those who've failed to make the judges shortlist so far are going all out. Joining the judges is author of the WI centenary cookbook Mary Gwynn.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The banquet is finally here. It's the culmination of months of hard work as the winning chefs strive to plate up perfect dishes for the ladies of the Women's Institute, at London's historic Draper's Hall where pioneers of the WI once gathered.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their summery starters. A dish celebrating childhood summer memories takes on a complicated courgette dish and a technically tricky take on the cucumber sandwich.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more intense as all the chefs take risks with their dishes. Mike Reid, Executive Chef at M Restaurants in London, shocks with when he pairs his fish with some highly unusual flavours.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's a smoke filled kitchen as the three chefs prepare their summer themed mains in the hope of impressing the veteran judge. Selin Kiazim is in her element, cooking a feast inspired by the summer barbecues she enjoyed with her family.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are feeling the pressure as they attempt their technical summer desserts. With only the two highest scorers going through to tomorrow's regional final, who will be sent home?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges: nd for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs have been challenged to create outstanding dishes that capture a `taste of summer'. Their menus must reflect the tastes, smells and colours of everybody's favourite time of year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and with a seafood specialist in the kitchen the competition is stepping up a gear. Tom is hoping he'll be able to elevate the humble mackerel to the standards required for the Wimbledon banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With a refined summer barbecue going up against a traditional Cornish speciality and a historic Wimbledon related recipe, who will come out on top? Andy Clatworthy is using his passion for historical cooking to reinvent a dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are honouring Wimbledon and the British summer with their technically challenging strawberry desserts. Only the two highest scorers will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs compete for a place in the national finals. To get there they'll have to impress the formidable panel of judges: Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom Parker is eager to impress with a salad that includes 10 elements. Meanwhile, Paul Askew, hopes his traditional techniques will triumph over his younger competitors, and Ellis Barrie is taking a risk with a creative cucumber and oyster starter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen gets even tenser as the three newcomers begin to understand the perfection and skill the veteran chef demands. Ellis Barrie attempts to create the flavour of a summer barbecue.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and the pressure is intense in the kitchen. In the hope of impressing the veteran judge, Paul Askew is serving an extremely personal main course based on his mother's favourite food.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Paul can't afford to make any mistakes and is pushing himself with a technically challenging baked alaska.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. The guest judge is Greg Rusedski, former British number one, who understands the high standards expected at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning banquet champion Tommy Banks takes on two ambitious newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington. They start by finding out whois the surprise veteran judge and who will be marking their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated, the three chefs are cooking their fish courses. Josh Overington, creates a decadent dish inspired by the prestige of the Wimbledon Championships.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are cooking their main courses and are all taking risks to try and win a place at the banquet. Danny Parker, is trying to recreate the flavours of an American barbecue but the veteran chef is dubious about one of his ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. All the chefs are using unusual ingredients in their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the North East must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver, and guest Judy Murray.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Scottish regional heats and returning chefs to the competition Michael Bremner and Ally McGrath battle ambitious newcomer Angela Malik for a place in the national finals. This time they are cooking their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated and the veteran chef is expecting perfection from the fish course. Ally McGrath creates an inventive dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Angela Malik is in her element, cooking a tiffin box based on memories of summer picnics. Michael Bremner is taking a huge risk, and Ally is creating an inventive dish based on a summer barbeque.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Angela Malik is cooking a technically challenging dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two Scottish chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver. They are joined by guest judge Gary Parsons.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Determined newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie take on returning chef Phil Carmichael for a place in the national finals. It's Phil's third time in the competition and this year he's determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the main course and all three chefs are cooking Welsh lamb in hope of impressing. Phil is taking a risk with barbecued lamb and in an attempt to elevate a humble barbecue to banquet level, he's serving it with many accompaniments.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the dessert course and the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges so all three chefs are feeling the pressure. Phil Carmichael creates a tennis ball dessert that combines two British classics, Eton mess and trifle.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledon's Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledon's Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Remember Pearl Harbor Narrated by Tom Selleck
programme.desc: On December 7, 1941, Japanese planes dropped torpedoes and bombs on an American Pacific fleet at Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: The Triunfante: Discovering an 18th Century Warship
programme.desc: Underwater archaeologists retrieve one of the biggest wrecks ever discovered in Spain, the Triunfante, and prepare it for display in a museum.

programme.title: Heroes on Deck: World War II on Lake Michigan
programme.desc: The US Navy trained nearly 15,000 pilots on Lake Michigan from 1942-1945, resulting in the sinking of more than 100 classic World War II fighters and dive bombers.

programme.title: Operation Oblivion
programme.desc: In the darkest days of World War II in the Pacific, an elite British military team sought to facilitate espionage and sabotage behind enemy lines. This now famous group, the British Special Operations Executive or MI6, faced a critical obstacle.

programme.title: Wings: World War Two in the Skies
programme.desc: In the Second World War, a new generation of planes and daredevil pilots take aerial combat to deadly new heights.

programme.title: Wings: World War Two in the Skies
programme.desc: In the Second World War, a new generation of planes and daredevil pilots take aerial combat to deadly new heights.

programme.title: Angle of Attack
programme.desc: Technological innovations in U.S. naval aviation change the face of war.

programme.title: Angle of Attack
programme.desc: Technological innovations in U.S. Navy aviation change the face of war.

programme.title: The Misty Experiment: The Secret Battle for the Ho Chi Minh Trail
programme.desc: A look at the US Air Force squadron whose pilots volunteered to search for enemy supply transports and anti-aircraft installations concealed within the web of trail paths and waterways collectively known as the Ho Chi Minh Trail.

programme.title: Battle of Okinawa: Operation Iceberg
programme.desc: Insight into the Battle of Okinawa, the largest assault of the Pacific campaign.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: By early 1944, Hitler had long been expecting an Allied attack on north-western France, but his problem was knowing when and where it would come. This episode takes a look at the Allied operations Bodyguard and Overload.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Concentrates on Hitler's attempts in the latter end of 1944 to shift the balance of power back in his favour.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The war in the Pacific against Japan was a savage hand-to-hand, island-to-island struggle, fought by new specialist fighting units like the valiant Chindits.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Allied forces converge on Germany, uncovering the horrors of Belsen and Buchenwald, before pushing towards Berlin, where Hitler clings to power in his last desperate days.

programme.title: War Comes to America
programme.desc: Director Frank Capra's World War II "Why We Fight" series ends with this portrait of the people.

programme.title: Tanks of World War Two: The T-34
programme.desc: James Holland explores the T-34, a tank built in the Soviet Union during the Second World War.

programme.title: Tanks of World War Two: The T-34
programme.desc: James Holland explores the T-34, a tank built in the Soviet Union during the Second World War.

programme.title: Tanks of World War Two: The Cromwell
programme.desc: Military historian James Holland takes a closer look at the British A27M Cromwell Tank produced during 1944-1945. Cromwell has not enjoyed the same post-war reputation as the German Panzer or Russian T-34.

programme.title: Tanks of World War Two: The Cromwell
programme.desc: Military historian James Holland takes a closer look at the British A27M Cromwell Tank produced during 1944-1945. Cromwell has not enjoyed the same post-war reputation as the German Panzer or Russian T-34.

programme.title: Dreadnought Destruction: Sinking the German Battle Fleet
programme.desc: German High Seas Fleet Admiral Ludwig von Reuter orders the sinking of his entire fleet in Orkney, Scotland, as the Treaty of Versailles is under negotiation.

programme.title: The Misty Experiment: The Secret Battle for the Ho Chi Minh Trail
programme.desc: A look at the US Air Force squadron whose pilots volunteered to search for enemy supply transports and anti-aircraft installations concealed within the web of trail paths and waterways collectively known as the Ho Chi Minh Trail.

programme.title: Battle of Okinawa: Operation Iceberg
programme.desc: Insight into the Battle of Okinawa, the largest assault of the Pacific campaign.

programme.title: Traitor/Patriot
programme.desc: Vladimir Vetrov was a KGB officer who worked for the French intelligence service. The "Farewell Document," shared with President Reagan included Soviet intelligence gained by Vetrov.

programme.title: Traitor/Patriot
programme.desc: While a prisoner in North Korea, George Blake was recruited by the Soviets to spy on the British. He was caught and sentenced to prison, but escaped to the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits the heart of the English countryside to explore Arundel Castle and the dramatic history of the Earls of Arundel.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Edinburgh's handsome hilltop bastion is Scotland's most popular paid tourist attraction. With a slew of sieges to its name, it is no surprise that the site boasts some formidable defences, and Dan puts one heavy duty cannon through its paces.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the history of Cardiff castle over more than 2,000 years and reveals how it became transformed from a strategically important Roman and Norman stronghold to a lavish fairy-tale Victorian home.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: In medieval England, the North was ruled from York. Dan Jones explores the history of its castle, from the Viking Jorvik and the Normans, through the dark days of the anti-Jewish pogrom and the imprisonment of the Knights Templar, to one of its most colourful inmates - the legendary highwayman Dick Turpin.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstruction, Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of the beautiful Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon family's manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house, a Georgian mansion and an elegant 20th-century retreat for the rich and famous.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits Lancaster Castle to explore its grisly history of crime and punishment, including the infamous Lancashire Witches trials and the martyrdom of the Jesuit priest Saint Edmund Arrowsmith.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion travel the country meeting developers hoping to make some money from their auction buys. Today they are in Stoke, Croydon and Warrington hearing tales from the auction rooms.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former doctor's surgery in need of TLC is converted into a family home in Houghton-le-spring and a flat in a 1930s block of flats in Edgware and a detached house in a woodland garden in Ashford in Kent are also given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion looks round a large Victorian terrace in Plumstead, Martin is in Mickleover in Derby hearing a father and daughter's plans for a flat and Lucy discovers what happened to a plot of land she saw five and a half years ago.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: There's a family home in Consett in County Durham, a rental investment in Derby and an auction buy in Beckenham in London which will be used to run a new business.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a bungalow in a stunning setting in the Lake District while Dion has a look at a flat close to Nottingham city centre. Lucy meets a woman who mistakenly thought she was buying a piece of land in Folkestone in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks at a flat in Kilburn in London with one of the worst cases of damp she has ever seen, while Martin and Dion are in the Midlands looking round semis with potential.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat with huge potential in London's Earlsfield, and terraced houses in Wombourne near Wolverhampton and Ynyswen near Treorchy in Wales are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is impressed with the potential of two shops and three flats in Tonypandy in Wales while Dion looks round a two-bedroom maisonette in Dawley and Lucy meets a first time developer with ambitious plans for an end terrace in London's Forest Gate.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-floor one-bedroom flat in London's Stepney Green, a three-bed semi in Dartford in Kent and a one-bedroom flat on the 17th floor of a block in Salford are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s bungalow in Dereham in Norfolk, a first-floor flat in London's East Ham and a property in Maryport in Cumbria with a hidden room are all sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans for their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a semi in Ilkeston in Derby, a flat in Streatham Hill in London and a bungalow in Somerset's Portishead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a fifth-floor flat in Greater Manchester, a three bed semi in Carlisle and three commercial buildings near Stanstead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An old Derbyshire farmhouse in Wirksworth gets a stunning make over, a developer builds a pair of semis in Chatham in Kent while a mid-terraced house in Blackhall Colliery in County Durham is given a makeover ready for the rental market.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet father and daughter developers. An apartment in a former mill in Mow Cop on the Staffordshire /Cheshire borders, a three-bed semi in East Ham and a flat in Bournemouth are all given makeovers with the help of Dad.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple about to get married buy their first home in Newcastle, a new dad thinks developing a flat in Leyton in London will give him more time with his four-month-old son, and a former IT worker develops a property in Gainsborough in Lincolnshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat over two floors in London's Camberwell, another flat in Telford in Shropshire and a three-bed semi in Alvaston in Derby are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a grand five-bedroom period house in Folkestone in Kent, a two bedroom flat in London's Leytonstone and a two bedroom mid terrace in Fenton in Stoke on Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A studio flat in a former 1960's office block which is grade two listed in London's Elephant and Castle, a terraced house in Pontygwaith in South Wales and a flat in in Cradley Heath near Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with no floor in Edgbaston in Birmingham, a detached house on several levels in Oldham and a former garage complex ripe for development in Forest Hill in London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A converted 16th century chapel in Matlock in Derbyshire, a two bedroom flat in Whetstone in London and a water damaged house in Wishaw in Scotland are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two bed semi in Meir in Stoke on Trent is given a country house look, a flat in London's Willesden Green also gets the wow factor and a semi in Dudley in the West Midlands loses it's 1980's look to be brought into the 21st century.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet property developers who have all bought at auction and hope to make a profit on their investments. Today a landscape gardener tackles a terrace in Ilkeston in Derby, two friends take on a four storey house in Sittingbourne in Kent while another couple of friends' first venture together is a studio flat in South Norwood in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Gravesend in Kent and Shipley in Derbyshire are given a new lease of life while former church premises in Harrow Weald in Middlesex are given a new use for the community.

programme.title: Death by Fame
programme.desc: A young actress gets her big break in the hit movie "Diary of a Wimpy Kid: Rodrick Rules"; within months, she turns up dead in her school parking lot; police sift through hundreds of hours of surveillance to attempt to catch her killer in the act.

programme.title: Who the Hell Did I Marry?
programme.desc: When Isaiah Carvalho meets NYPD officer Valerie Cincinelli, he thinks his dreams have come true. He soon learns she's not only cheating but also hired a hitman to kill him.

programme.desc: With first-hand testimony from family members, police officers, witnesses and reporters, we shine a light on some of Britain's darkest murder locations, examining the crimes that rocked these communities.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: With first-hand testimony from family members, police officers, witnesses and reporters, we shine a light on some of Britain's darkest murder locations, examining the crimes that rocked these communities.

programme.desc: The quiet seaside town of Mablethorpe was an unlikely location for what appeared to be an organised hit on a couple in their 50s. Emilia travels to the Lincolnshire coast to learn about the violent murders of Joan and John Stirland in 2004.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: The story of Louise Wigan, who vanished aged 15 in 1995. The next day, her body was found in a field near her home, leading to a hunt for a killer in Appley Bridge.

programme.title: Murdertown

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Katherine Kelly examines the 1986 murder of 16-year-old Elaine Doyle. One of Scotland's longest-running murder enquiries, it took 26 years to find her killer.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: My Stepdad Abused Us for Twelve Years
programme.desc: Albert Maughan subjected Louise and Craig Turnbull to a harrowing regime of abuse starting from when they were only 3 and 4 years old. 24 years later, Louise decides to put this monster behind bars once and for all.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: A Twins' Story: Our Uncle Raped Us
programme.desc: Identical twin sisters Rachel and Tracy were raped by their uncle John McCallum when they were just 5-years-old. The abuse lasted over a period of eight years until they were 13. In 2012 they broke almost 30 years of silence.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.desc: Follows shocking true life stories of the darkest and most sickening acts. No subject is off limits.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.desc: In this special episode, we look back over five years and 40 episodes of Britain's Darkest Taboos and examine what we can learn from some of the most disturbing familial crimes in British criminal history.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: My Sister: Driven To Her Death By Her 'Safe Bet' Husband

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: Shadows of Death
programme.desc: In 1993, a family is torn apart when a mother in Jacksonville, Florida goes missing. The case stumps detectives, who believe that the truth about what happened to her is locked inside the mind of the missing woman's three-year-old son.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: Shadows of Death

programme.title: Faking It: Tears of a Crime

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the Griffins inherit a mansion, Peter bids $10 million on a piece of art, losing his new home.

programme.title: Family Guy
